 以下を含む１つ以上のベクター：
Ｉ．第１のＣＲＩＳＰＲ−ＣＡＳ系キメラＲＮＡ（ｃｈｉＲＮＡ）をコードするヌクレオチド配列に機能的に連結された調節エレメントであって、前記第１のＣＲＩＳＰＲ−ＣＡＳ系ｃｈｉＲＮＡは、 
 （ａ）トリヌクレオチドリピート伸長を有する細胞内で第１の標的ＤＮＡ配列にハイブリダイズ可能であり、第１のＣＲＩＳＰＲ複合体の前記第１の標的ＤＮＡ配列への配列特異的結合を誘導可能である、第１のガイド配列、
 （ｂ）第１のｔｒａｃｒ ｍａｔｅ配列、及び
 （ｃ）第１のｔｒａｃｒ配列、を含み、
 （ａ）、（ｂ）、及び（ｃ）が５’から３’の方向に配列されている、調節エレメント、および、
 第２のＣＲＩＳＰＲ−ＣＡＳ系ｃｈｉＲＮＡをコードするヌクレオチド配列に機能的に連結された調節エレメントあって、前記第２のＣＲＩＳＰＲ−ＣＡＳ系ｃｈｉＲＮＡは、 
 （ｄ）前記細胞内で第２の標的ＤＮＡ配列にハイブリダイズ可能であり、第２のＣＲＩＳＰＲ複合体の前記第２の標的ＤＮＡ配列への配列特異的結合を誘導可能である、第２のガイド配列、
 （ｅ）第２のｔｒａｃｒ ｍａｔｅ配列、及び
 （ｆ）第２のｔｒａｃｒ配列、を含み、
 （ｄ）、（ｅ）、及び（ｆ）が５’から３’の方向に配列されている、調節エレメント、および 
ＩＩ．ＣＲＩＳＰＲ酵素をコードするヌクレオチド配列に機能的に連結された調節エレメント、ここで、前記ＣＲＩＳＰＲ酵素はＣａｓ９である、 
 ここで、前記第１および第２の標的ＤＮＡ配列は、共に、ＣＴＧ、ＣＡＧ、ＣＧＧ、ＧＣＧ、または、ＧＡＡトリヌクレオチドリピート伸長を含むヌクレオチド配列に隣接し、

組成物。
 A composition for treating a disease or disorder caused by trinucleotide repeat elongation: One or more vectors, including : I. A regulatory element operably linked to a nucleotide sequence encoding a first CRISPR-CAS chimeric RNA (chiRNA), wherein the first CRISPR-CAS chiRNA comprises: (A) capable of hybridizing to a first target DNA sequence in a cell having trinucleotide repeat extension and inducing sequence-specific binding of the first CRISPR complex to the first target DNA sequence , The first guide arrangement, (B) a first tracr mate sequence, and (c) a first tracr sequence, Regulatory elements , wherein (a), (b) and (c) are arranged in the 5'to 3'direction , and A regulatory element operably linked to a nucleotide sequence encoding a second CRISPR-CAS chiRNA , wherein the second CRISPR-CAS chiRNA comprises (D) A second guide capable of hybridizing to the second target DNA sequence in the cell and inducing sequence-specific binding of a second CRISPR complex to the second target DNA sequence. Array, (E) a second tracr mate array, and (f) a second tracr array, A regulatory element, wherein (d), (e), and (f) are arranged in the 5'to 3'direction, and II. A regulatory element operably linked to a nucleotide sequence encoding a CRISPR enzyme, wherein the CRISPR enzyme is Cas9, Wherein the first and second target DNA sequences are both adjacent to a nucleotide sequence comprising CTG, CAG, CGG, GCG, or GAA trinucleotide repeat extension, Here, the first CRISPR complex comprises (1) a first guide sequence hybridizable to the first target sequence, and (2) the first guide sequence hybridizable to the first tracr sequence. The CRISPR enzyme complexed with the tracr mate sequence of Here, the second CRISPR complex comprises (1) a second guide sequence hybridizable to the second target sequence, and (2) a second guide sequence hybridizable to the second tracr sequence. The CRISPR enzyme complexed with the tracr mate sequence of including, Composition.

Recent advances in genome sequencing techniques and analytical methods have significantly accelerated the ability to classify and map genetic factors associated with a diverse range of biological functions and diseases. Precise genome targeting techniques allow for the systematic reverse engineering of causative gene variants by allowing selective perturbation of individual genetic elements, as well as synthetic biology, biotechnology and pharmaceutical applications. Needed to make progress. Genome editing techniques, such as designer zinc fingers, transcriptional activator-like effectors (TALEs), or homing meganucleases are available for producing targeted genomic perturbations, but are inexpensive, easy to set up, and scalable. There remains a need for new genomic engineering techniques that are easy to target at multiple locations within the eukaryotic genome.

The guide sequence can induce sequence-specific binding of the CRISPR complex to the target DNA sequence and alter expression of the genomic locus in the cell. The CRISPR complex mediates binding to, or cleavage of, double-stranded or single-stranded DNA, optionally inserting DNA, which allows the cell to edit a genomic locus. The CRISPR-Cas system can be a multiplexed CRISPR enzyme system further comprising multiple chimeras and/or multiple multiguide sequences and a single tracr sequence. The CRISPR-Cas system can minimize off-target activity as a multiplexed CRISPR enzyme system. The CRISPR enzyme can be nickase. The CRISPR enzyme can include one or more mutations. Any or all of a polynucleotide sequence encoding a CRISPR enzyme, a guide sequence, a tracr mate sequence, or a tracr sequence can be RNA, DNA, or a combination of RNA and DNA. In one aspect, the polynucleotide comprising a sequence encoding a CRISPR enzyme, a guide sequence, a tracr mate sequence, or a tracr sequence is RNA. In one aspect, the polynucleotide comprising a sequence encoding a CRISPR enzyme, a guide sequence, a tracr mate sequence, or a tracr sequence is DNA. In one aspect, the polynucleotide is a mixture of DNA and RNA, and some polynucleotides comprising one or more CRISPR enzyme encoding sequences, guide sequences, tracr mate sequences, or tracr sequences are DNA, Some polynucleotides are RNA. In one aspect, the polynucleotide comprising the sequence encoding the CRISPR enzyme is DNA and the guide sequence, tracr mate sequence, or tracr sequence is RNA. One or more polynucleotides containing sequences encoding CRISPR enzymes, guide sequences, tracr mate sequences, or tracr sequences can be delivered by liposomes, nanoparticles, exosomes, microvesicles, or gene guns.

When a polynucleotide is referred to when it is referred to as a polynucleotide and is said to "comprise" a feature, eg, a tracr mate sequence, it is to be understood that the RNA sequence includes this feature. When the polynucleotide is DNA and is said to "comprise" a feature, eg, a tracr mate sequence, the DNA sequence is or can be transcribed into RNA that comprises this feature. If the feature is a protein, eg a CRISPR enzyme, the DNA or RNA sequences mentioned are translated or capable of being translated (and in the case of the first transcribed DNA). Further, it is to be understood that when RNA encoding a CRISPR enzyme is provided to a cell, the RNA can be translated by the cell to which the RNA is delivered.

A first regulatory element, comprising: (b) a tracr mate sequence, and (c) a tracr sequence, and II. Encode a CRISPR enzyme comprising at least one or more nuclear localization sequences (or, optionally, at least one or more nuclear localization sequences, as some embodiments may not contain any NLS).
